plan.title: STL writer — merge base + pixels, emit single-color binary STL

narratives.Overview: Combine the two meshes from the geometry layer into a single-body binary STL on disk.
narratives.Acceptance: `qr2stl.writers.stl.write_stl(base: Mesh, pixels: Mesh, path: Path) -> None` writes a valid binary STL containing the merged triangles (base first, pixels second). The file opens cleanly in PrusaSlicer / Bambu Studio / OrcaSlicer / Blender without warnings. Reading the file back via numpy-stl yields the same triangle count as sum(len(base.vectors), len(pixels.vectors)). Deterministic: byte-identical output for identical input on repeated runs (same platform).
narratives.Scope: src/qr2stl/writers/stl.py — `write_stl()` uses numpy-stl's `Mesh.save(path, mode=Mode.BINARY)`. Merges via numpy concatenation on the data array. No header metadata beyond numpy-stl's default.
